seoTitle: How to Screen Record Using Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to screen record directly in Canva for tutorials, presentations, or training content, from setup to export.
intro: Recording your screen is a great way to make tutorials, walkthroughs, or presentation recordings, and Canva includes tools to do it without extra software. This tutorial walks through preparing, recording, and exporting a screen recording in Canva.

steps.heading: Navigate Canva's interface
steps.body: Locate the screen recording tool within Canva before you start capturing anything.

steps.heading: Prepare for screen recording
steps.body: Set up whatever you plan to show on screen, whether that's a presentation, document, or another application.

steps.heading: Record your screen
steps.body: Start the recording and walk through your content as if you were presenting live.

steps.heading: Export and share your recording
steps.body: Once finished, export the recording so it can be shared or embedded wherever you need it.

faq.question: Can I record both my screen and webcam at the same time in Canva?
faq.answer: Depending on the feature set available in your Canva plan, you may be able to combine screen and webcam capture.

faq.question: Is there a time limit on Canva screen recordings?
faq.answer: Time limits can vary based on your Canva plan, so check your account's specific limits before a long recording.

faq.question: Can I edit the recording after capturing it?
faq.answer: Yes, once recorded, you can bring the clip into Canva's video editor to trim or adjust it.

faq.question: Do I need to install any software to screen record in Canva?
faq.answer: No additional software should be needed, since the recording tool is built into Canva's platform.
